strategy_justification: Site is protected by Akamai Bot Manager. Direct HTTP returns 403 'Access Denied'. Standard Playwright is blocked. Only UC Chrome bypasses the detection. Fresh probe confirms uc_chrome_residential loads the page fully (48K+ chars, 2 JSON-LD blocks with Product block containing offers). The scraper must use SeleniumBase UC (undetected Chrome) as the browser strategy.
proxy_justification: ESCALATED from datacenter. The fresh probe confirms residential proxy is the method that works (uc_chrome_residential). Previous scraper attempt 1: datacenter returned 39-char blank pages. Previous scraper attempt 2: no-proxy got Akamai 403. Residential IPs are more trusted by Akamai's IP reputation system. The cached probe info said 'uc_chrome_datacenter' but the fresh live probe returned 'uc_chrome_residential' — using residential is verified by the live test.

title.note: h1 NOT FOUND in DOM. Confirmed via probe page title: 'C15 Super Booster | Brightening Booster | Paula's Choice'. Extract from JSON-LD Product.name.

price.test_result: offers block present in JSON-LD (exact price value needs runtime extraction)
price.note: JSON-LD offers=present confirmed by fresh probe. All CSS price selectors return NOT FOUND. Extract from JSON-LD Product.offers[0].price.

currency.note: Extract from JSON-LD offers.priceCurrency. paulaschoice.com is a US site.

availability.note: Extract from JSON-LD offers.availability. Value is a schema.org URL that needs mapping to human-readable text.

original_price.note: May be available if multiple offer items exist in JSON-LD or if highPrice/lowPrice are set. Needs runtime verification against actual JSON-LD content.

image.note: Extract from JSON-LD Product.image. May be string or array. Filter out non-product images.

description.note: DOM description selector NOT FOUND. Extract from JSON-LD Product.description. May contain HTML tags that need cleaning.

brand.note: DOM brand selector NOT FOUND. Extract from JSON-LD Product.brand.name.

sku.note: DOM sku selector NOT FOUND. Extract from JSON-LD Product.sku.

rating.note: DOM rating selector NOT FOUND. Extract from JSON-LD Product.aggregateRating.ratingValue.

reviews.note: Individual reviews available in JSON-LD Product.review array.

url.note: Extract from JSON-LD Product.url. Fallback to the input URL.

category.note: No BreadcrumbList JSON-LD found. Product.category may not exist.

location.note: No location/warehouse data found in JSON-LD or page structure.

block_2.note: Not useful for product data extraction. Skip this block.

scraper_workflow: 1. Start SeleniumBase UC Chrome with residential proxy (proxy_tier='residential')
2. WARMUP: Navigate to homepage (https://www.paulaschoice.com) FIRST using uc_open_with_reconnect
3. Wait 18 seconds on homepage for Akamai sensor data collection and _abck cookie establishment
4. Check for blank page (source_len < 100) — if blank, log error and skip
5. Detect block pages (Akamai, Cloudflare, CAPTCHA, generic) — if blocked, log and skip
6. Accept cookie consent if present (try common selectors and text content buttons)
7. THEN navigate to product URLs one by one using uc_open_with_reconnect
8. After each product URL navigation, wait INITIAL_RENDER_WAIT (5s) for JS framework to start
9. Early blank-page detection: if source_len < 100 and no title, return with remarks='PROXY_BLANK_PAGE'
10. Use WebDriverWait polling (up to 25s, poll every 1s) for JSON-LD script blocks to appear
11. Extract all script[type='application/ld+json'] blocks from rendered DOM
12. Parse JSON and find the Product type block (index 0, prefer block with offers)
13. Extract fields: name, description, image, brand.name, sku, mpn, offers.price, offers.priceCurrency, offers.availability, aggregateRating.ratingValue, aggregateRating.reviewCount, review[]
14. Map availability URL (https://schema.org/InStock) to human-readable text
15. Set url from JSON-LD or from the input URL
16. Set metadata fields: src_url, status_code, scraped_at, remarks

warmup_steps.cookie_selector: Look for common cookie consent buttons: button[class*='accept'], button[class*='cookie'], button[class*='consent'], [data-testid*='cookie'], #onetrust-accept-btn-handler, button[aria-label*='Accept']
warmup_steps.justification: Akamai Bot Manager requires sensor data collection on the homepage before allowing access to product pages. The scraper must visit the homepage first and wait for Akamai to establish _abck cookies. Without warmup, direct product page navigation triggers 403 or blank pages.

retry_adjustments.root_cause: The previous scraper failed because ALL proxy configurations could not connect: no-proxy got Akamai 403, datacenter and residential returned 39-char blank pages. However, the fresh probe CONFIRMS uc_chrome_residential loads the page (48K chars, JSON-LD with offers). The scraper code itself is excellent — the issue was purely connectivity. The discrepancy between probe success and scraper failure may be due to: (1) proxy credential/config differences between environments, (2) the probe's multi-layer Akamai bypass being more robust than simple SB UC + proxy, (3) transient proxy issues during the test run.

fixes_required.fix: Use residential proxy tier (already the default in previous scraper draft). The fresh probe confirms uc_chrome_residential works.
fixes_required.implementation: Ensure --proxy-tier residential is the default. The previous scraper already has this as default.

fixes_required.fix: Keep homepage warmup before product scraping. Visit https://www.paulaschoice.com first and wait 18 seconds for Akamai sensor data and _abck cookies.
fixes_required.implementation: Already implemented in previous scraper draft via warmup_session().

fixes_required.fix: Keep early blank-page detection after INITIAL_RENDER_WAIT. If source_len < 100 and no title, skip JSON-LD polling immediately.
fixes_required.implementation: Already implemented in previous scraper draft.

fixes_required.fix: Keep cookie consent handling during warmup.
fixes_required.implementation: Already implemented in previous scraper draft via ACCEPT_COOKIES_JS.

retry_adjustments.what_changed: Proxy escalated from datacenter to residential (fresh probe confirms uc_chrome_residential works). The previous scraper code is well-written and needs no code logic changes — only the proxy tier needs to be residential (which was already the default). If residential proxy still returns blank pages in browser-service, it may be a proxy infrastructure issue (credentials, routing) rather than a scraper code issue.

previous_scraper_code_review.extraction_logic: CORRECT — JSON-LD parsing, field mapping, error handling all well-implemented
previous_scraper_code_review.warmup_logic: CORRECT — Homepage visit, 18s wait, cookie consent, blank-page detection
previous_scraper_code_review.proxy_handling: CORRECT — build_proxy_url with configurable tier, no-proxy flag
previous_scraper_code_review.error_handling: CORRECT — Block detection, diagnostic logging, graceful failure
previous_scraper_code_review.js_snippets: CORRECT — All use var-based syntax (not arrow IIFEs) for Selenium execute_script
previous_scraper_code_review.note: The scraper code from the previous attempt is complete and correct. The only issue was connectivity/proxy. The code should be reused as-is with residential proxy.

escalation_note: Proxy escalated from datacenter to residential (attempt 2 of 3). Fresh live probe confirms uc_chrome_residential loads the page successfully (48K chars, JSON-LD Product block with offers). If residential proxy still fails in browser-service with blank pages, it would indicate a proxy infrastructure issue (credentials/routing in browser-service container) rather than a scraper code issue. Max 3 attempts total; after residential fails, the job ends.
confidence_notes: Medium-high confidence on strategy and extraction (probe verifies uc_chrome_residential works, JSON-LD has all fields including offers). Medium confidence on whether the scraper will successfully connect — the probe works in its environment but the previous scraper couldn't connect with any proxy tier. The scraper code is excellent and needs no changes. The uncertainty is entirely about whether residential proxy will work in the browser-service container.
